# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for VPC with multiple subnets for our microservices architecture. Configure route tables, internet gateway, and NAT gateways. Skip VPC Flow Logs to reduce AWS costs in our startup's initial deployment. We'll enable logging after we secure seed funding.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

# CloudFormation template for creating a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with multiple subnets and route tables, Internet Gateway & NAT gateway 
Resources:  
    VPC1:                  # name of the resource as per AWS naming convention.
        Type : 'AWS::EC2::VPC'      # Resource type in EC2 (Not used if you only declare a physical server) - Can be omitted when not declaring an instance     OR  In this case, using it to set up VPC properties and define the Subnets within our template.
        Properties:                  # Object holding all resource options defined by user or default in AWS CloudFormation templates     
          EnableDnsSupport : True            # DNS ENABLEMENT - TRUE (default) OR False  [true, false]           If set to true this allows the VPC Subnets and Internet Gateway that use DNS for route propagation. Defaults: None(FALSE), Allows VPN connections only if enabled in network interface
          EnableDnsHostnames : True            # ENABLEMENT OF HOSTNAMES (default) - TRUE [true, false] If set to true enables all possible hostname formats – example hosts are myserver.us-west-2a.vpc.amazonaws.com for instance in EC2
          Tags:                          # Container where user defined metadata is stored       Key : Value   Any additional tags you want added that we do not otherwise declare here, can be used when declaring a resource     ie - CreatedBy : "Managed by Terraform" (default)  For example if the name of this object already exists in another part or stack then it may get updated as per user's request
            Key: Name                     # Required Tag key       [string]        e.g., 'Name', required for deletion, not available when declaring an instance     Value : VPC-Project   You can use any existing tag name in this list that you want to associate with your resources (default) 
            Description: "My Virtual Private Cloud"    # Required Tag description [string]        e.g., 'Description'       If not specified, it defaults as empty string and updates the one passed after creation ie - CreatedBy : Managed by Terraform   For example if you want to create a tag named Description with value My Virtual Private Cloud then user needs only pass this info during resource declaration
            DeletionPolicy: Retain               # Default Delete policy [string]        e.g., 'Retained'     (default)  Can be one of the following values - Determined by deployment and cannot be changed after setup   You can select if you want AWS to destroy & delete all resources when destroying stack or not
            ResourceArn: !Sub arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:${AWS::Region}:012345678910/vpc    # If set, it uses this ARN instead of creating a new one. Defaults : None  [string]
          CidrBlock: '10.0.0.0/16'           # Required Subnet - Define the IP address range that your VPC will use for launching resources in   e.g., subnets[].CidrBlock="SubnetA", "SubnetB". [string]
          EnableRouteTable: True             # ROUTE TABLE ENABLEMENT FOR NEWLY CREATED SUBNETS - TRUE (default) OR False  Can be omitted when not declaring an instance     Allows automatic creation and setup of routes that use this subnet. Defaults : None(TRUE), Creation only
          VPCName: 'MyVPC'                   # Required name for the vpc [string]        e.g., "Main-Project", required when you are declaring resources in another stack  (default)   For example, if your resource list has a different naming scheme then user needs to provide this
          EnableInternetGateway: True     # Internet GateWAY ENABLEMENT - TRUE [true] Can be omitted with no internet gateway OR False.[false] If the subnet should have an associated route table. Defaults : None(TRUE), Creation only when enabling NAT GATEways  
          TagsAllowed:                        # Allows all existing tags to pass through if not provided or user wants override, must be in format Key=Value    [string]  If a new resource needs its own set of associated metadata then this tag field is required. For example 'CreatedBy = "Managed by Terraform"'
           VPCID: !Ref ${AWS::StackId}        # The ID for the existing AWS Account and Region, it will be used to create all resources in our stack [string]  (default)   Defaults : None. Set this only when you are declaring an instance using Subnet definitions
    PublicSubnets:                      # Define multiple public sub networks inside VPC with different CidrBlocks and availability zones     Key       Value      Allows the user to define several separate custom set of resource options  [map]   ie - 'PublicSubnets' : { "Key" : "Value", ... },
    PrivateSubnets:                      # Define multiple private sub networks inside VPC with different CidrBlocks and availability zones     Key       Value      Allows the user to define several separate custom set of resource options  [map]   ie - 'PrivateSubnets' : { "Key" : "Value", ... },
    RouteTables:                      # Defining route tables for Public & Private subnet connections. Can also be used as reference by name or ID     Key       Value      A single key-value pair to specify a resource property value [map]   ie - 'PublicRouteTable' : { "Key" : "Value", ... },
    InternetGateway:                    # Required internet gateway for the VPC if not set previously in physical server OR user needs new IG setup. Can be omitted with no Internet GateWAY created     Key       Value      If already exists, use this ARN else create a fresh one  [string]  
    NATInstanceType: 'eip-auto'           # Required EIP Auto Allocation - For auto allocation of Elastic IPs. Can be omitted if static allocations are used or in cases where you do not have an associated public instance     e.g., "eip" OR Specify the type by passing it as string i.e , 'nat'
    NATInstance:                      # Required EIP Auto Allocation - For auto allocation of Elastic IPs for internet gateway   Key       Value      If already exists, use this ARN else create a fresh one  [string]
